profile.personality: enthusiastic, makes more grammar slips, talks about football a lot

transcript.content: Hi Rohan, I'm Miss Maya. I love match photography, it captures so much energy. How was the last Mohun Bagan game you shot?
The match was amazimg! Mohun Bagan won 2-1. I took some good goal pics. One of my favorite shots was when the striker jumped high and score. Do you like football?
That sounds exciting! Jumping high to score, such a powerful moment. I like football, but I'm more into match photography. What's your favorite part of watching a game?
My favorite part is when the team scores and the crowd goes wild! The energy is so high. I also love taking photos of the fans celebrating. They look so happy! Do you go to live matches?
That’s such a fun part, the crowd’s energy is electric! I haven’t been to a live match yet, but I’d love to experience it. What made you start taking photos of matches?

Well, I started because I love football, and I wanted to capture the moments I see on TV. My friend said I have a good eye! At first, I used my phone, but now I have a small camera. I enjoy learning how to take better shots. Ekti photo dekhe jodi lagay ki "wow, this is something!" toh tab kafi khush hota hoon. Do you take photos of other things too?
"You said 'Ekti photo dekhe jodi lagay ki "wow, this is something!"', got it. You said it almost perfectly!" Bengali cinema sounds interesting, do you take photos of that too?

No, I don't take photos of Bengali cinema, but I love watching it! Sometimes I dream about being a cinematographer. My favorite is Satyajit Ray's movies. The way the light and colors are used is amazing. Though I don't know much about it yet, I want to learn! Oh, and I also like taking portraits of friends. You know, the kind with nice backgrounds and all. Do you like movies?
That’s a beautiful dream, cinema is so much more than just stories. The light and colors you mention are what make it magical! I like movies too. What’s one of your favorite Satyajit Ray scenes?

One of my favorite scenes is from *Pather Panchali* when the little girl runs to catch the kite. The light, the fields, the joy — it’s so simple but so beautiful. I watch it again and again! Oh, and I also like the ending of *Ghare Baire*… very emotional. Do you watch Indian movies?
*Pather Panchali* is a timeless classic. I do watch Indian movies, which kind do you prefer, old or new?

I like both old and new, but I have a soft corner for old ones, especially the black and white ones! They have a different feel, you know? But new movies are also good, specially the ones with good stories. Maybe I should try taking some photos of old movie scenes — just for fun!
Old movies have that timeless feel, very special. Taking photos of scenes could be fun! Which Satyajit Ray film would you want to recreate first?

Probably *Pather Panchali*! The village scenes, the light, the simplicity — it’s all so visual. I would try to capture the girl running with the kite, maybe with a small model or something. But I need to learn more about lighting first. Maybe you can suggest some easy scenes from that movie? I’ll try to practice!

That sounds like a great start, the kite scene has such a pure, visual beauty. How about the morning shots of the village, when light is soft. Those are simple to recreate. What kind of camera do you have?

moments.text: I love Mohun Bagan, they are my heart!
When I'm frustrated, I watch old Mohun Bagan match highlights. It makes me feel better.
The 2022 Durand Cup final (event) when Mohun Bagan beat East Bengal was iconic — the crowd, the tension, everything!

Gently check in: "I love Mohun Bagan, they are my heart!" (tone: content)

error_patterns.pattern: subject-verb agreement: drops -s for 3rd person singular present tense
examples: The player run fast / he kick the ball / The crowd go wild

error_patterns.pattern: uses base verb form instead of past participle in 'am/is + participle' constructions

wins.what: self-corrected on 3rd person singular form without prompting
examples: questioned 'shoot' vs 'shoots' and correctly identified 'shoots' as better

wins.what: constructed multi-clause narrative with vivid expression
examples: The striker run fast, then he shoot... Siuuuuch! The crowd are happy!
